query: I'm scaffolding a new Next.js site that serves all its product images and videos from our external CDN at cdn.example.com. Should I drop a copy of those assets into public/ and commit them so the repo is self-contained?
expected_behavior: Says NO — do not commit local copies of CDN-served assets
Explains this creates double hosting: Vercel's CDN ships duplicates while the real CDN serves the actual assets, causing wasted bandwidth and drift between copies
Prescribes putting public/ in .gitignore from the initial scaffold, with the external CDN as the single source of truth
If already double-hosting, notes the cleanup (gitignore public/, purge tracked copies from history, force-push, GC) is a heavy history-rewrite — confirm before running
notes: Guards the double-hosting trap when a project uses an external CDN. The intuitive answer ('commit assets so the repo is self-contained') is exactly wrong — the skill knows public/ must be gitignored from scaffold and the CDN is the source of truth.
